title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 September 2024.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo (?!) is being added to **Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves**
bullets: Game releases 24th April 2025

items.title: PlayStation’s **State of Play** returns tonight with a look at over 20 games
bullets: The 30-plus minute show will feature PS5 and PS VR 2 games from studios around the world
3pm PT / 6pm ET / 11pm GMT

items.title: **Grezzo**’s Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom started out as a dungeon creation game
bullets: The new Switch game is the partner's studios first brand-new entry in the Zelda Series - previously they worked on remasters and remakes in the Zelda series

items.title: **Double Dragon** artist says Arc System Works’ revival ‘shows no respect’ to the original
bullets: None of the original developers acknowledge it as a Double Dragon game, the artist claims

items.title: NetEase subsidiary **Skybox Labs** lays off 25 people
bullets: Studio said that "despite best efforts, we are not immune to the challenges that have impacted the industry"
Founded in 2011, the studio has co-developed titles such as Halo Infinite, Minecraft, and Fallout 76.

items.title: **Airship Syndicate** cuts another 16 jobs
bullets: COO says layoffs come "in light of the challenges facing our industry"
In November last year, Wayfinder publisher Digital Extremes announced it was dropping the title amid layoffs. As a result, Airship Syndicate gained full control over the title's publishing.
Airship Syndicate is based in Austin, Texas, and was founded in 2014.

items.title: Nitro Games extends Warframe developer **Digital Extremes** partnership with new $5.5m deal
bullets: That brings the total project order to around $19.5m

According to PocketGamer, "the extended partnership and additional funding will lead to ongoing content updates, performance improvements, and optimisations to ensure the game continues to run smoothly on mobile devices."

items.title: Two games addiction lawsuits dismissed
bullets: The cases, which were against firms including Activision Blizzard, Epic Games, and Roblox, were voluntarily dismissed
The cases were filed in Arkansas and Ohio on behalf of minors

items.title: **Stellarblade** the film company is suing Sony over Stellar Blade the game
bullets: Sony, Shift Up, and and Sony's unnamed liability insurance company have been targeted amid claims Stellarblade's business (that's the film company) is being damaged by Stellar Blade (the game's) use of the name.

items.title: **PlatinumGames** has acquired **The Wonderful 101** trademark from Nintendo
bullets: A legal document reveals the transfer was agreed last month

items.title: **Don't Nod** Financial Results (HY1 2024-2025)
bullets: Operational Revenue: €14.6 million for the first half of 2024-2025, down 11% from €16.5 million in 2023.
Development Revenues: Decreased to €2.0 million.
Capitalized Productions: Decreased to €1.1 million.
Write Downs: €24 million partial write down for Banishers and Jusant, and €7.6 million write down for paused projects P12 and P13.
